name: Modjeskas
recipeInstructions:
Allow one full hour for preparation and follow instructions exactly! Do not attempt on a damp and rainy day.
1. Boil the sugar and white Karo syrup until it reaches 254°F.
2. Add the margarine and stir until melted, keeping the mixture boiling.
3. Keep the syrup boiling as you add the evaporated milk gradually, stirring constantly.
4. Add a pinch of baking soda and continue to boil and stir until the mixture reaches 237°F (soft ball stage).
5. Pour the caramel into a buttered pan without scraping the sides.
6. Allow the caramel to cool until it is comfortable to touch.
7. Cut the caramel into squares measuring approximately 1 inch each.
8. Stretch each square of caramel flat enough to fold around a marshmallow, or wrap in wax paper squares.
9. Alternatively, cut the caramel into smaller pieces and dip in chocolate, or simply cut and wrap as caramels.
These caramel-wrapped marshmallows are native to Louisville, Kentucky and are absolutely wonderful! Worth every stir.
